summary_strategy: Reframe Conner as a reliability-focused engineer who builds and operates secure, scalable infrastructure—positioning his DevSecOps rigor, fleet automation, and systems thinking as directly transferable to Supabase’s hosted Postgres platform

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize C, Rust, Python, SQL—highlighting low-level systems languages and query language first; keep all 11 items
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to emphasize endpoint management, vulnerability orchestration, and CI/CD-integrated tooling; add 'managed database security' as a conceptual bridge
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Reorder to surface RAG pipelines, vector databases, and NLP first; add 'database query intent classification' as a plausible extension of security log analysis work
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to highlight Flask, FastAPI, Docker—emphasizing API-first, containerized services; add 'managed service patterns' as a conceptual link
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to lead with infrastructure as code, container orchestration, and release automation; add 'idempotent deployment patterns for distributed systems' as a safe conceptual extension
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder PostgreSQL first, then managed databases (Snowflake, Databricks), then operational DBs (Redis, ClickHouse); keep all 10 items—do not rename or merge

experience_focus.must_highlight: idempotent automation at scale, operational reliability for distributed systems, secure API-driven tooling
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 2,900-user portal, 500 hours annually, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no 'designed PostgreSQL internals', no 'built database extensions', no speculative metrics like '99.99% uptime'

risk_controls: Do not invent PostgreSQL-specific experience—frame database familiarity through operational patterns (IAM, secrets, managed services)
Replace all speculative technical depth claims with transferable DevSecOps behaviors (e.g., 'operational familiarity' instead of 'deep expertise')
Never claim extension development unless explicitly stated—C/Rust usage must be tied to existing security tooling work
